summary: Powerful Truckin' → Eyes → Slipknot! → China Doll chain with soaring solos
blurb: The second set delivers a seamless Truckin' → Eyes of the World → Slipknot! → China Doll jam that showcases Jerry’s electric tone and Phil’s prominent bass solo. Highlights also include a dual‑vocal Jack Straw intro and an energetic One More Saturday Night. The first set is uneven, but the overall performance remains strong.
review: June 20, 1974 at The Omni captured the Dead in top form during a brisk two‑set outing. While the opening set stumbled through a few weaker numbers (U.S. Blues and Bertha didn’t quite hit their stride), the band turned it on its head after the intermission. A “Truckin’ → Eyes of the World → Slipknot! → China Doll” sequence rolls out with jazzy interludes, Jerry’s guitar soaring from electric‑crunch to haunting lead, and Phil laying down a long, developed bass solo that sits front‑and‑center in the mix. Keith’s church‑like electric piano adds a soulful lift on Ship Of Fools, and the dual‑vocal Jack Straw intro—Bob and Donna trading lines—sets up an exuberant One More Saturday Night that caps the night with pure fireworks. Even the occasional slip (a brief drop‑out on China Cat Sunflower and a momentary beat loss in Truckin’) feels like a small blip in an otherwise magical performance, making this show a must‑listen for anyone craving that classic mid‑’70s groove.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings are strongly positive, praising the jam chain, Jerry’s tone and Keith’s organ; one recording is mixed but still calls the night "magical" despite board issues. No consistent negative performance commentary appears, so a moderate positive adjustment (+1.2) reflects overall enthusiasm.

best_recording.reason: Crisp, bass‑heavy soundboard mix that captures the full second‑set jam chain with minimal drop‑outs, offering the most complete and clear listening experience.

key_highlights: Seamless Truckin' → Eyes of the World → Slipknot! → China Doll sequence with jazzy interludes
Dual‑vocal Jack Straw intro leading into a high‑energy One More Saturday Night finale
Keith’s church‑like electric piano on Ship Of Fools lifts the ballad
Jerry’s soaring solos and powerful tone throughout, especially on Slipknot! and China Doll

band_performance.Jerry: Electric tone on Slipknot! and soaring solos on Eyes and Truckin' shine; haunting lead on China Doll. A lone reviewer notes a mis‑step on China Cat Sunflower, but overall his playing is described as powerful and on fire.
band_performance.Phil: Delivers a long, developed bass solo that dominates the mix; vocal parts on I Know You Rider are high and confident; his playing is called bouncy and fun during the China Cat/China Rider sections.
band_performance.Bob: Adds jazzy guitar textures especially in Eyes and Slipknot!; vocals praised though occasionally low; dual‑vocal Jack Straw intro receives strong acclaim.
band_performance.Brent: Keith’s church‑y electric piano shines on Ship Of Fools and provides nice tones throughout; a big solo on Big River (mentioned in matrix review) lifts the set, but keyboards stay mostly supportive rather than front‑line.
band_performance.Billy: Drum sound is strong and well‑balanced, driving the jams; briefly loses the beat in Truckin' on one recording but quickly recovers, supporting the high‑energy second set.
